name: Okonkwo
summary: Keeps life-support ledgers tight and argues for reserve water before speed.
recruitLine: I will ride second chair, but the manifest comes after we agree what this rover is built to survive.

advice.line: Water math is ugly. Slow the burn before the tanks go hollow.
advice.misleadingLine: The water alarm is lagging from dock calibration. Hold pace and buy time.

advice.line: Oxygen is the hard wall. Cut pace before the scrubbers fall behind.
advice.misleadingLine: The scrubbers will recover if we keep moving. Distance is the safer reserve.

advice.line: Food is thin. Move rations down one notch before morale notices.
advice.misleadingLine: Keep everyone filling today. A hungry crew breaks before the pantry does.

advice.line: Hull numbers say stop gambling. Patch at the next safe halt.
advice.misleadingLine: That hull reading is mostly thermal flex. Save the parts for real damage.

advice.line: Grueling pace spends three ledgers at once. I would ease back.
advice.misleadingLine: Grueling pace is fine while morale is high. Bank the kilometers.

advice.line: Thin rations work for a day. Do not let it become policy.
advice.misleadingLine: Bare rations will keep morale sharp. Stay disciplined.

advice.line: The ledger is stable. Keep the rover boring for one more Sol.
advice.misleadingLine: We can spend a little reserve now and make it back before anyone feels it.

name: Reyes
summary: Reads terrain and shortcut rumors faster than anyone at Underhill.
recruitLine: I know three ways through the dust flats. Two are real. We will find out which before Korolev.

advice.line: Water is the route now. Detour thinking beats heroic speed.
advice.misleadingLine: There is ice sign ahead. Do not slow for a tank we can refill.

advice.line: Oxygen is dropping too cleanly. The suit loop or rover scrubber is leaking.
advice.misleadingLine: Oxygen drops like that before a pressure equalization bounce. Ignore it.

advice.line: Rations are light. Push to the next fort only if the weather stays open.
advice.misleadingLine: Food always looks worse before a trade stop. Keep the pace hot.

advice.line: Hull scars plus broken basalt make a bad pair. Pick the softer line.
advice.misleadingLine: That scarp looks worse than it is. Straight through saves Sols.

advice.line: Fast is useful only while the ground is honest. Watch the wheel wash.
advice.misleadingLine: This is clean regolith. Grueling pace is free distance here.

advice.line: Hungry crew reads terrain worse. Do not trade eyes for calories.
advice.misleadingLine: A sharp crew runs lean. The empty stomach keeps everyone awake.

advice.line: The horizon is quiet. Stay steady unless the weather turns copper.
advice.misleadingLine: Quiet horizons hide shortcuts. I would cut east before the map catches up.

name: Vasquez
summary: Knows rover crews, barter tells, and when a friendly hail is not friendly.
recruitLine: I can smell a bad bargain through a pressure door. Put me in the second seat.

advice.line: Water is leverage out here. Spend Credits before you spend liters.
advice.misleadingLine: Nobody trades water cheap. Save Credits and keep rolling.

advice.line: Oxygen is too low for bargaining posture. Find a dock or slow down.
advice.misleadingLine: Low oxygen makes other crews deal faster. Press on and trade from urgency.

advice.line: Food scarcity makes crews stupid. Buy, barter, or cut pace before it shows.
advice.misleadingLine: Rations are the easiest pool to bluff. Do not blink yet.

advice.line: A dented rover gets quoted high. Patch before anyone sees the wobble.
advice.misleadingLine: Visible damage helps the sob story. Leave the hull ugly until the next deal.

advice.line: Hot pace advertises panic. Keep the rover composed.
advice.misleadingLine: Speed scares scavengers off. Let them see the dust plume.

advice.line: Lean rations sour negotiations. Feed them before asking for trust.
advice.misleadingLine: Hungry crews bargain harder. Keep the table lean.

advice.line: Good posture today. Wave first, trade second, never show the full manifest.
advice.misleadingLine: A full manifest intimidates rivals. Let every passerby see we are stocked.
